evidence_text: The study analyzes 10 repositories and 124 pull requests under paired conditions with and without AGENTS.md.
source_excerpt: “We analyze 10 repositories and 124 pull requests, executing agents under two conditions: with and without an AGENTS.md file.”
note: This is the stated study scope, not a claim about generality beyond the sampled material.
caveat: This is the stated study scope, not a claim about generality beyond the sampled material.

evidence_text: With AGENTS.md present, median wall-clock runtime is reported as 28.64% lower.
source_excerpt: “Our results show that the presence of AGENTS.md is associated with a lower median runtime (Δ 28.64%).”
note: The abstract reports association within the study design.
caveat: The abstract reports association within the study design.

evidence_text: Median wall-clock time falls from 98.57 seconds without AGENTS.md to 70.34 seconds with AGENTS.md.
note: This is the tabled median comparison for the paired runs.
caveat: This is the tabled median comparison for the paired runs.

evidence_text: The wall-clock time difference is marked as statistically significant by Wilcoxon signed-rank test at p < 0.05.
source_excerpt: “∗ Statistically significant difference, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (p < 0.05).” The asterisk is attached to “Wall-Clock Time (s).”
note: The source reports significance for this metric only as shown in the table.
caveat: The source reports significance for this metric only as shown in the table.

evidence_text: With AGENTS.md present, median output token consumption is reported as 16.58% lower.
source_excerpt: “Our results show that the presence of AGENTS.md is associated with ... reduced output token consumption (Δ 16.58%).”
note: The abstract states reduced output token consumption, not reduced total token consumption across every token category.
caveat: The abstract states reduced output token consumption, not reduced total token consumption across every token category.

evidence_text: Median output tokens fall from 2,925.00 without AGENTS.md to 2,440.00 with AGENTS.md.
note: This is the tabled median comparison for output tokens.
caveat: This is the tabled median comparison for output tokens.

evidence_text: The output-token difference is marked as statistically significant by Wilcoxon signed-rank test at p < 0.05.
source_excerpt: “∗ Statistically significant difference, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (p < 0.05).” The asterisk is attached to “Output Tokens.”
note: The source explicitly marks statistical significance for output tokens in Table 1.
caveat: The source explicitly marks statistical significance for output tokens in Table 1.

evidence_text: Applying the root-only AGENTS.md constraint reduced the repository set from 132 repositories to 89.
source_excerpt: “In this study, we focus on the simplest configuration: repositories that contain one AGENTS.md file only at the repository root... Applying this constraint yields 89 repositories (from 132 total).”
note: This is a dataset construction step used to reduce confounding from multiple instruction files.
caveat: This is a dataset construction step used to reduce confounding from multiple instruction files.

evidence_text: After content-based filtering and manual verification, the study retained 26 repositories with qualifying root AGENTS.md files.
source_excerpt: “The classification of AGENTS.md files was performed using an LLM (gpt-oss-120b) according to the above criteria... followed by manual verification of the filtered results. After applying this filtering step, we retain 26 repositories, each containing a qualifying root AGENTS.md file.”
note: The retained set depends on both LLM classification and manual verification.
caveat: The retained set depends on both LLM classification and manual verification.

evidence_text: Selected pull requests had to be merged code-changing PRs with total additions plus deletions of at most 100 lines, no more than 5 modified files, and creation and merge dates after AGENTS.md was introduced in the repository.
source_excerpt: “Each PR satisfies the following criteria: (1) Size constraint: total additions + deletions ≤ 100 LoC; (2) Scope constraint: ≤ 5 modified files; (3) Status: merged PRs only; (4) Temporal constraint: PR created and merged after the introduction of AGENTS.md in that repository; (5) Change type constraint: PR modifies code files only.”
note: These constraints narrow the task set to smaller, code-only PRs.
caveat: These constraints narrow the task set to smaller, code-only PRs.

evidence_text: For PRs lacking usable descriptions, the task statement was generated with a local gpt-oss-120b model using the PR diff and the pre-merge repository structure.
source_excerpt: “To provide consistent and informative task input, we generate a GitHub-issue-style task statement for each PR using a local LLM (gpt-oss-120b). The model is prompted with: (1) the PR diff (patch) and (2) the repository structure at pre-merge state (e.g., file tree).”
note: This standardizes task inputs across PRs that lacked sufficient natural-language context.
caveat: This standardizes task inputs across PRs that lacked sufficient natural-language context.

evidence_text: The paper did not perform a full correctness or functional-equivalence evaluation; it instead reports a manual sanity check on 50 randomly sampled PR tasks for non-empty, non-trivial outputs.
source_excerpt: “We analyze 10 repositories and 124 pull requests, executing agents under two conditions: with and without an AGENTS.md file.”
note: This is the stated study scope, not a claim about generality beyond the sampled material.
caveat: This is the stated study scope, not a claim about generality beyond the sampled material.
